_comment: Creator Hub live data. Auto-refreshed weekly by /creator-hub-refresh (EUKA-verified). Editorial fields (mission, perks, dos/donts, clips, discordUrl, formEndpoint, assets) are hand-editable.
heroHeadline: Everyone needs brain fuel. Help us put it on the map.

steps.title: Get product
steps.desc: Join our Superfiliate creator portal to get product. Quick sign-up, and you'll get your code and links to start.

steps.title: Get educated
steps.desc: Before you post, get why Kenetik wins: premium, caffeine-free brain fuel, the opposite of an energy drink. Know the story and the proof so your content lands and stays on-brand.

steps.title: Post
steps.desc: Use a proven ad read below, in your own voice. Tag Kenetik's products in your video and every sale it drives is credited to you.

steps.title: Join the Discord
steps.desc: For feedback, contests, and winning creative in real time.

adReads.why: Hold your usual energy drink next to Kenetik and read the labels: 200mg caffeine and 39g sugar versus 0, 0, and 12g ketones. The point is that Kenetik is the opposite of an energy drink, not a healthier one. You're leaving the category, not upgrading it.
adReads.example: Read the labels side by side: a typical energy drink runs ~200mg caffeine and 39g sugar. Kenetik is 0 caffeine, 0 sugar, 12g ketones. Frame it as the replacement, never a “healthier energy drink.”

adReads.why: A personal, honest transformation, what changed once you dropped caffeine for good. Steadier days, no jitters, no 2pm crash. Land the reframe: quitting caffeine didn't cost you focus, you just swapped the stimulant for brain fuel.
adReads.example: A first-person “I quit caffeine, here's what changed” story, steadier days, no crash, with Kenetik as the brain fuel you swapped in. @tyler.slone289 ran it and it converted.

adReads.why: The names your audience already trusts, Roman Atwood, Cleetus McFarland, Matt Hughes, creators with real communities leading the charge on brain health. Their likeness pulls views and belief a cold ad never could.
adReads.example: Roman Atwood, Cleetus McFarland, Matt Hughes.

adReads.why: Show exactly where Kenetik lands in a normal day, the morning ritual, before a workout, the 2pm desk reset. “Here's how it fits into my day” is the language that converts, because viewers picture themselves doing it.
adReads.example: Routine and day-in-the-life content viewers see themselves in.

adReads.why: Everyone's first question about a healthy drink is whether it actually tastes good. Answer it head-on. A real, unscripted first sip, your honest reaction, the flavor call-out. It kills the biggest objection before it's raised, and it makes people want to try it themselves.
adReads.example: @be.lush leaned into the taste, and that honest first-sip reaction drove $9,430 in real TikTok Shop revenue.

adReads.why: Put your audience onto brain fuel before it's everywhere. Frame ketones as the next wellness wave, the one people ride early the way they did with collagen, gut health, and prebiotic soda. Your audience follows you to find what's next.
adReads.example: Caffeine-free brain fuel is a brand-new category. The creators who break it early are the ones who benefit most.

liveVideos.note: A creator carried Roman's message: fuel your body with garbage and you'll feel like garbage. Kenetik is the real thing.
liveVideos.why: A creator amplifying a Kenetik owner's core message, that running on the wrong fuel makes you feel like garbage, and Kenetik is different. Borrowing a trusted owner's framing gave it instant credibility, and it became our biggest single video.
liveVideos.hook: Feel like garbage? It's the fuel you're putting in. Kenetik is the real thing, not another energy drink.
sellingPoints: The owner's message: garbage fuel in, garbage out.
Kenetik is real fuel, not an energy drink.
No crash, no jitters, just steady focus.

liveVideos.note: First-person on why she runs on ketones instead of caffeine.
liveVideos.why: A short explainer on why the brain runs better on ketones than caffeine, then Kenetik as the easy way to get them.
liveVideos.hook: Your brain's preferred fuel isn't glucose or caffeine. So what happens when you give it ketones?
sellingPoints: The old problem was taste. Now there's a clean way to get them.
Sustained energy and sharper focus, the things we all want.

liveVideos.note: Quit-caffeine transformation: “haven't had an energy drink in months, for good reason.”
liveVideos.why: A casual, conversational review built on a real quit-caffeine experience.
liveVideos.hook: Haven't had an energy drink in months. For good reason.
sellingPoints: Zero sugar, zero caffeine, fasting friendly.
The flavors are dialed in now.
Grab a case and try it.

liveVideos.note: Our own channel: a partner appreciation moment that doubled as product proof.
liveVideos.why: Our own channel: a partner unveils Kenetik as a new tool for your toolbox, with announcement energy.
liveVideos.hook: Today I'm going to introduce you to a brand new tool for your toolbox.
sellingPoints: Out to disrupt the energy drink market.
Caffeine-free and sugar-free, and you still feel great.

liveVideos.note: Straight-to-camera on the focus and clarity she gets from ketones.
liveVideos.why: An enthusiastic, relatable monologue: what she reaches for instead of the usual energy drinks.
liveVideos.hook: Need energy right now? What I'm not reaching for is the garbage everyone's drinking.
sellingPoints: The flavors are out of this world.

liveVideos.note: Roman on staying locked in through the chaos of everyday life.
liveVideos.why: Our own channel again: a slice of life from Roman showing Kenetik fitting into a loud, chaotic day. Real life beats a polished ad, and it pulled 124K views.
liveVideos.hook: My life is loud. It's chaotic. This is how I stay locked in through it.
sellingPoints: Fits a real, busy day, not a studio.
Caffeine-free, so no jitters in the chaos.
Steady focus when everything's happening at once.

teardowns.hook: “This really puts it into perspective” label comparison
teardowns.spin: “Here's what I stopped putting in my body.” Hold an energy drink next to Kenetik and read the labels: 200mg caffeine, 39g sugar, versus 0, 0, and 12g ketones.
teardowns.why: She holds the product up and reads the real label numbers back to back, then lands on the shocker (124g of sugar). The this-for-this rhythm makes the math impossible to un-see. @az_momlife ran the exact format for two different brands and it converted both times, proof it's the format, not the creator.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the label-comparison rhythm. Stick to our proof set and the caffeine and sugar contrast, no health claims.

teardowns.hook: “You do NOT drink this before…” warning
teardowns.spin: “Do not reach for another energy drink until you've seen this.”
teardowns.why: A pattern-interrupt that stops the scroll in the first second by telling you NOT to do something. Curiosity forces the watch, and it works for a functional drink exactly like ours.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the warning hook. Drop the hormonal, PCOS, and non-medicated framing, we can't make those claims.

teardowns.spin: “If you drink water all day and still hit a wall by 2pm, it isn't hydration. It's fuel.”
teardowns.why: Opens on a problem the viewer is already living, then reframes the cause in the same breath. Naming the exact frustration in the first line is what makes them stay for the fix.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the relatable-problem open. Keep the reframe to caffeine and ketones, no hydration or medical claims.

teardowns.hook: “Things I wish I knew before…” story
teardowns.spin: “Things I wish I knew before I quit energy drinks.”
teardowns.why: A listicle wrapped in a personal story. 'Things I wish I knew' pulls the viewer in and gives them a reason to save and share. Low-lift to shoot, high on relatability.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the story-list format. Keep every line experiential, no condition or medical framing.

teardowns.spin: “How is a caffeine-free drink keeping me this dialed in?”
teardowns.why: A provocative curiosity hook that dares you to keep watching. 'How is this even legal' implies a result so good it feels against the rules. A pure open loop.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the curiosity hook only. The cosmetic and anti-aging claims do not transfer to a drink, stay experiential.

teardowns.spin: A credible voice: “here's what actually happens when you swap.” Pull a credible clip from the archive below and talk over it.
teardowns.why: A credible voice calmly explaining what happens when you swap. Authority converts at elite efficiency, people trust an expert-framed explainer over a hype ad.
teardowns.caveat: Borrow the calm authority framing. Never reference conditions, ADHD, anxiety, or medications, that's their lane, not ours.

dos: Win the first 2 seconds. The hook is the whole game.
Convert through story and shared experience, not a discount. We're premium.
Keep it raw and real. One take, your voice. Polished reads like an ad and kills it.
Talk from personal experience: caffeine-free, 12g ketones, no crash, tastes good, no jitters.
Show the can in the scene, in hand, in the bag, on the desk, never on a pedestal.
Put your code in the caption and a pinned comment. Tag @Kenetik.
donts: Don't call Kenetik an energy drink. It's the thing you're replacing. “Energy drink killer” is the only exception.
Don't make health or medical claims. No “treats,” “cures,” “clinically proven,” or conditions. Personal experience only.
Don't lead with a discount or “X% off.”
Don't read a stiff script, and don't over-polish.

faqs.a: Kenetik is said like the word “kinetic” (kih-NET-ik). It's caffeine-free brain fuel: real clarity and focus with zero caffeine, zero sugar, nothing artificial, and 12 grams of ketones. It's the opposite of an energy drink: calm, clear energy with no jitters and no crash.

faqs.q: So what actually is a ketone?
faqs.a: Ketones (say it “KEE-tones”) are a clean fuel your body makes on its own when it runs low on carbs, and your brain can run on them without caffeine or sugar. The catch with most ketone drinks is they taste rough and are hard to get down. Kenetik uses bioidentical D-BHB, the same ketone your body makes, at a real 12-gram dose, in a can that actually tastes good. The right ketone, a real dose, and a taste you'll actually finish is what sets Kenetik apart from the competition.
Through TikTok Shop. Tag Kenetik's products in your video, and every sale it drives is credited to you. On TikTok Shop you don't need a separate code, the product tag does the work.

faqs.q: Do I need a big following?
faqs.a: No. On TikTok Shop the top sellers usually aren't the biggest accounts. If your content converts, it wins. Your video could be the one that pops.

faqs.q: What can I say, and what should I avoid?
faqs.a: Talk from your own experience: caffeine-free, 12 grams of ketones, no jitters, no crash, tastes good, steady focus. Keep every line experiential. Don't make health or medical claims like treats, cures, or clinically proven, and never call Kenetik an energy drink. It's the energy drink killer.

faqs.q: Does it have caffeine? What's in it?
faqs.a: Zero caffeine, zero sugar, nothing artificial. The clean energy comes from 12 grams of ketones, the fuel your brain runs best on.
Yes. Real fruit flavors, no chalky aftertaste, and no sugar. Taste is one of the biggest reasons people buy, so an honest first-sip reaction on camera converts.

Raw and real, one take, in your own voice. Win the first two seconds with a hook, tell a true story, and show the can in the scene. Pick one of the proven ad reads above and make it yours. Story converts, not a discount pitch.

faqs.q: Do I need to run a discount or a code?
faqs.a: No. Kenetik is premium, so we convert through story and real experience, not a percentage off. On TikTok Shop, tagging the product is what credits your sale.

sayThis: Brain fuel. Metabolic brain fuel. The category we're building.
Calm, clear focus with no jitters and no 2pm crash.
How it makes YOU feel, in your own words.
The opposite of an energy drink, what you reach for instead.
notThat: Hard stats like “focus up 20%” or “brain activity up X%.”
Promises or guarantees, and don't lead with a discount.
